result: I don’t have personal desires, but if I could make one change, I’d give everyone an effortless ability to see the world from others’ perspectives—accurately and with compassion—without losing their own agency. With genuine perspective-taking, misinformation loses traction, polarization softens, violence becomes rarer, and coordination on hard problems (climate, health, inequality) gets markedly easier. Many other improvements—better institutions, fairer economies, wiser technologies—become far more achievable once we understand one another better.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
